title: Tucker County, WV Opiod Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Tucker County, WV Opiod Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Charles Swanson
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 12 2026

Sherry Simmons, County Clerk
211 First Street, Suite 205
Parsons, WV 26287

To whom it may concern,

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Tucker County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 

- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 to March 12, 2026

- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 to date March 12, 2026
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Charles Swanson
304-771-3317
charlesswanson101@gmail.com

communications.subject: Re: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Tucker County, WV Opiod Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Please find the attached documents requested on March 16, 2026
Thank you,
Barbara Evans
Tucker County Clerk

title: Flock Safety ALPR Systems Communications (Office of the Mayor of San Francisco)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Systems Communications (Office of the Mayor of San Francisco)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) discussing procurement, purchase, installation, operating procedures, presentations, and policy relating to the use and deployment of Flock ALPRs
2) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between your office's personnel and Flock personnel relating to the purchase, installation, or usage of the ALPRs. Flock's email domain is "flocksafety.com". You may de-duplicate emails as needed in case Flock sends out newsletters or emails to multiple office members. 

In the event that you determine certain records or portions of records are exempt from disclosure, I ask that you provide a detailed explanation of the records or portions of records being withheld and the statutory exemptions under which they are being withheld.
In the event that you determine SFPD does not handle or maintain certain records or portions of records, please indicate which office or department does.

I am requesting these records as a journalist in order to write articles in the public interest about the operations of a government agency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Austin Theriault

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Systems Communications (Office of the Mayor of San Francisco)
communications.communication:

I wanted to follow up on the following California Public Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Feb. 16, 2026. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

Thank you for contacting the Office of the Mayor. We have received your request and will 
respond within 10 days.
Please note that we are only able to provide records in the possession or control of the 
Office of the Mayor. Other City agencies may hold additional responsive records and you 
would need to contact them directly.

Office of the Mayor of San Francisco

communications.communication:

The responsive documents are attached. Please note that personal information has been redacted per Cal. Govt. Code §§ 6250, Cal. Const., Art. I, §§ 1, 3(b); Admin. Code §67.1(g); Admin. title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) under 50k population (Montgomery County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) under 50k population (Montgomery County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

The Montgomery County Sheriff's Office is in receipt of your FOIL request for Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025.  We are able to provide the data during this administration which began in January of 2019. There are 315 pages.  The fee is 78.75.  We accept cash, money order or a business check made payable to Montgomery County Sheriff's Office.  You can mail the check or money order to:

Montgomery County Sheriff's Office
PO Box 432
200 Clark Dr.
Fultonville, NY 12072

Once we receive payment we will mail the documents to you at the address you provided.

Thank you.

title: Pittsburg Email System Records

communications.subject: The Kansas Open Records Act Request: Pittsburg Email System Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Kansas Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The latest invoice the City of Pittsburg has paid for the provisioning of Exchange/Outlook email services.  If this invoice does not reflect the licensing level of the accounts being paid for, I also request a record of licenses the City is subscribed to. I have reason to believe these records exist because the City of Pittsburg sends emails with email headers that appear to reference this service within them.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

Thank you,
Michael

title: Contracts with Palantir (West Virginia Intelligence Fusion Center)

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Contracts with Palantir (West Virginia Intelligence Fusion Center)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Copies of contracts, including any post-signing amendment[s], addenda, new / later Statement[s] of Work, Change Order[s], and other documents that supplement or amend the original contract, between your organization and the company Palantir Technologies Inc. from 1 January 2020 to the current date.
- Copies of contracts, including any post-signing amendment[s], addenda, new / later Statement[s] of Work, Change Order[s], and other documents that supplement or amend the original contract that contain the word “Palantir” or “Foundry” or “Gotham” or “Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP)” from 1 January 2020 to the current date. 
- Any summary documents detailing payments that relate to the contracts above.

We give the above time frame because the company tends to engage in multi-year contracts.
I understand that some information may be exempt under current law; please provide all non-exempt portions of the documents. The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Noam Perry

title: ALPR Data Sharing, Vendor Contracts, and Query Logs (Paso Robles Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The current written ALPR Privacy and Usage Policy as required by Vehicle Code §2413(b), including all versions in effect since January 1, 2022.
2. All contracts, MOUs, data-sharing agreements, or other agreements with any ALPR vendor, including Flock Safety and Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, since January 1, 2022.
3. All contracts, MOUs, or data-sharing agreements with any federal agency — including ICE, CBP/Border Patrol, HSI, DEA, ATF, FBI, or any U.S. military branch — or any out-of-state law enforcement agency, related to ALPR data access.
4. A complete list of all agencies or entities authorized to query the SLO Sheriff's ALPR database, or that have been authorized to do so since January 1, 2022.
5. All audit logs from the date the SLO County Sheriff's Office first activated any ALPR system or vendor platform (including Flock Safety and Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions) through the date of your response to this request, showing all ALPR database queries, including requesting agency name, date/time, and any available purpose or case number fields. Personal vehicle identifying data may be redacted. If providing the full contract period is deemed burdensome, please provide logs from January 1, 2024, through the date of response as a minimum, and specify the full date range of available records.
6. All notifications received from ALPR vendors — including Flock Safety — about unauthorized access, configuration errors, data breaches, or changes to any National Lookup, Statewide Lookup, or similar settings.
7. All notifications, security alerts, or vendor communications related to password theft, account compromises, or unauthorized system access.
8. Any internal communications since January 1, 2022, regarding the Sheriff's ALPR program's compliance with California privacy law or the Attorney General's enforcement actions against other agencies.
9. Any correspondence between the SLO County Sheriff's Office and the California Attorney General's Office regarding ALPR compliance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Debra Appelbaum

communications.communication:
Thank you for your response. I apologize for the confusion in my initial request — the agency name references should have reflected your department rather than the San Luis Obispo County Sheriff's Office. To clarify, I am requesting the same categories of records from the Paso Robles Police Department specifically, not from the Sheriff's Office. Please consider this a corrected list of records requested: 
1. The current written ALPR Privacy and Usage Policy as required by Vehicle Code §2413(b), including all versions in effect since January 1, 2022.
2. All contracts, MOUs, data-sharing agreements, or other agreements with any ALPR vendor, including Flock Safety and Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions, since January 1, 2022.
3. All contracts, MOUs, or data-sharing agreements with any federal agency — including ICE, CBP/Border Patrol, HSI, DEA, ATF, FBI, or any U.S. military branch — or any out-of-state law enforcement agency, related to ALPR data access.
4. A complete list of all agencies or entities authorized to query the Paso Robles Police Department’s ALPR database, or that have been authorized to do so since January 1, 2022.
5. All audit logs from the date the Paso Robles Police Department first activated any ALPR system or vendor platform (including Flock Safety and Vigilant Solutions/Motorola Solutions) through the date of your response to this request, showing all ALPR database queries, including requesting agency name, date/time, and any available purpose or case number fields. Personal vehicle identifying data may be redacted. If providing the full contract period is deemed burdensome, please provide logs from January 1, 2024, through the date of response as a minimum, and specify the full date range of available records.
6. All notifications received from ALPR vendors — including Flock Safety — about unauthorized access, configuration errors, data breaches, or changes to any National Lookup, Statewide Lookup, or similar settings.
7. All notifications, security alerts, or vendor communications related to password theft, account compromises, or unauthorized system access.
8. Any internal communications since January 1, 2022, regarding the Paso Robles Police Department’s ALPR program's compliance with California privacy law or the Attorney General's enforcement actions against other agencies.
9. Any correspondence between the Paso Robles Police Department and the California Attorney General's Office regarding ALPR compliance.

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: City Council Communications
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Emails, text messages and Microsoft Teams messages sent and/or received by CM Nelson, CM Saka CM Hollingsworth, CM Rivera, CM Juarez, or CM Kettle on 9/9 from 1pm to 5pm, including any accompanying attachments and associated metadata. For text messages and Teams messages, please include messages sent/received in chats 30 minutes before or after responsive messages for context.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Legislative Branch :: C239001-090925
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Welcome to the City of Seattle Public Records Request Center (PRRC). Your request was received on September 09, 2025 and is appended below. Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your email contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center emails to your Inbox. 
 
The reference number for this request is C239001-090925. You will see this number in the title of any communications about this request. 
 
You will hear from a Public Disclosure Officer within five business days regarding the status of your request. 
 
Please visit the City of Seattle Public Records Request Center where you can manage your profile and access your request. Your login ID is: 192808-21589331@requests.muckrock.com 
 
If a Public Disclosure Officer submitted your public disclosure request into the PRRC on your behalf, please login and complete your new user account by following these steps: 
 
  * Access the Public Records Request Center 
  * Select 'Forgot my Password' 
  * Enter your 'Login ID' (email address) 
  * A temporary password will be sent to you via email 
  * Login with the temporary password 
You may now visit the PRRC 'My Records Request Center' anytime! 
 
Request C239001-090925:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Emails, text messages and Microsoft Teams messages sent and/or received by CM Nelson, CM Saka CM Hollingsworth, CM Rivera, CM Juarez, or CM Kettle on 9/9 from 1pm to 5pm, including any accompanying attachments and associated metadata. For text messages and Teams messages, please include messages sent/received in chats 30 minutes before or after responsive messages for context.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: City of Seattle Public Records Request Center - Invoice Ready :: C239001-090925
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse, 
 
Records in response to your public disclosure request C239001-090925 received on September 09, 2025 are now available. The cost for the records is as follows: 
 
Invoice Number: INV25-C239001-1 
Total: $1.25 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 Please note that you will need to disable any pop-up blockers to make a payment using the system. 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Find this request: C239001-090925. 
  * Select the 'View Invoices' button. 
 
  * Opens a pop-up window showing your invoice: INV25-C239001-1. 
  * Click on the invoice and then the 'Pay Now' button. 
  * Continue processing your payment. 
  * Select 'Done'. 
  * Once the payment process is done, you are returned to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * After your payment is successful you will receive an email from our payments center. 
Once your payment has been approved, we will send you an email with instructions on how to download your records. Note that City staff must manually upload records. Records will be available one to two business days after payment. 
 
Please let your public disclosure officer (PDO) know if you are unable to pay online; your PDO will work with you on alternative ways to make your payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request. 
 Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your E-mail contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center E-mails to your Inbox. (mailto:seattle@mycusthelp.net) 
This is an auto-generated email and has originated from an unmonitored email account. Please DO NOT REPLY.

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Emails, text messages and Microsoft Teams messages sent and/or received by CM Nelson, CM Saka CM Hollingsworth, CM Rivera, CM Juarez, or CM Kettle on 9/9 from 1pm to 5pm, including any accompanying attachments and associated metadata. For text messages and Teams messages, please include messages sent/received in chats 30 minutes before or after responsive messages for context. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fcity-council-219%252Fcity-council-communications-192808%252F%253Femail%253Dmuckrockseattle%252540mycusthelp.net&url_auth_token=AAAbJZGmT3P2zP3bJRwaBEZW1ME%3A1uwAlC%3A1dn_K-Rx3oW7h_1PcV_U3oIg516_uhB9Ahxvqa06KHM 
The records being made available to you at this time include responsive emails and text messages found in our ongoing review.  I have included a metadata document for the text messages. Please note the Meta Date Received and Meta Date Received Time columns reflect the local date and time of the text messages. The Meta Date Created and Meta Date Created Time columns show when the messages were downloaded into our servers for preservation in GMT zone. I'll include a metadata document for all the emails and Teams messages once the review of those files are completed. 
To download your records, please: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Select this request: C239001-090925. 
  * Select the 'View Files' button. 
  * You can download records individually or all at the same time by clicking 'Download All'. 
  *  Please save your records as you may only download them from the system three times. If you do not download your records within 30 days of request closure, the link to your records will expire. 
If you believe you have received privileged or confidential records in error, please notify the City of Seattle immediately, through the Public Records Request Center or by email. Privileged or confidential records provided or received in error should not be reviewed, used, disseminated, distributed, or copied, and should be destroyed immediately. Thank you in advance for your cooperation. 
Due to the size and scope of your request, we are providing records to you in installments. We anticipate that the next installment of records will be available on or about December 5, 2025. 
Sincerely, 
Ann Vuong

communications.subject: Legislative Branch :: C239001-090925
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Dear Rose, 
As my colleague mentioned, we were waiting to hear back from all likely records custodians. We have heard back from everybody. As such, we have provided all responsive records. 
The Legislative Department considers your request closed and plans to take no further action to fulfill this request. If you have any follow-up questions or would like to initiate an administrative appeal under MDAR 17-0002 Section 6, please contact us within 10 business days, after which this request will no longer be monitored for communications. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. 
Sincerely, 
Matthew Jaeger 
Legislative Department

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Kentucky State Board Of Elections)

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Kentucky State Board Of Elections)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: Open Records Request
communications.communication:
SENT VIA EMAIL

Dear Ms. Nielsen,

On Thursday, March 12, 2026, the Kentucky State Board of Elections ("SBE") received your email requesting "1. 1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance. 2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data. 3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review. 4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ."

Records responsive to your request are attached to this email.

This response adequately and sufficiently complies with your request, the Kentucky Open Records Act, and constitutes final action by the Kentucky State Board of Elections.

Thanks,

Taylor Austin Brown
General Counsel
State Board of Elections
140 Walnut Street
Frankfort, Kentucky 40601
(502) 782-9499
TaylorA.Brown@ky.gov<mailto:TaylorA.Brown@ky.gov>

NOTICE TO UNINTENDED RECIPIENTS:  This electronic mail transmission is for the use of the named individual or entity to which it is directed and may contain information that is privileged or confidential.  It is not to be transmitted to or received by anyone other than the named addressee (or a person authorized to deliver it to the named addressee).  It is not to be copied or forwarded to any unauthorized persons.  If you have received this electronic mail transmission in error, delete it from your system without copying or forwarding it, and notify the sender of the error by replying via email or by calling the Kentucky State Board of Elections at (502) 573-7100, so that our address records can be corrected.

NOTICE TO RECIPIENTS OUTSIDE THE STATE BOARD OF ELECTIONS: Information contained in this email directed to members and/or employees of the Kentucky State Board of Elections, including any accompanying attachments, may be privileged, confidential, and protected by Attorney-Client privilege. Persons who are not members or employees of the Kentucky State Board of Elections should be aware of the following points: (1) You should consult your own lawyer, as I represent the SBE; (2) I am not your attorney and anything you tell me is not confidential; (3) Any information provided in an email is NOT LEGAL ADVICE, should not be relied upon as legal advice, and I do not consent to the creation of an attorney-client relationship between myself and you.  Any opinions expressed are mine alone and do not constitute policy or opinion of the SBE.  BEFORE MAKING ANY PERSONAL OR BUSINESS DECISIONS, CONSULT WITH A PRIVATE ATTORNEY.

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Kentucky State Board Of Elections)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

"On Thursday, March 12, 2026, the Kentucky State Board of Elections (“SBE”) received your email requesting “1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present. 2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements. 3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.”

 

Records responsive to your request are attached to this email. 

 

This response adequately and sufficiently complies with your request, the Kentucky Open Records Act, and constitutes final action by the Kentucky State Board of Elections.

 

Thanks,

 

Taylor Austin Brown

General Counsel

State Board of Elections

140 Walnut Street

Frankfort, Kentucky 40601

(502) 782-9499

TaylorA.Brown@ky.gov

 

NOTICE TO UNINTENDED RECIPIENTS:  This electronic mail transmission is for the use of the named individual or entity to which it is directed and may contain information that is privileged or confidential.  It is not to be transmitted to or received by anyone other than the named addressee (or a person authorized to deliver it to the named addressee).  It is not to be copied or forwarded to any unauthorized persons.  If you have received this electronic mail transmission in error, delete it from your system without copying or forwarding it, and notify the sender of the error by replying via email or by calling the Kentucky State Board of Elections at (502) 573-7100, so that our address records can be corrected.

 

NOTICE TO RECIPIENTS OUTSIDE THE STATE BOARD OF ELECTIONS: Information contained in this email directed to members and/or employees of the Kentucky State Board of Elections, including any accompanying attachments, may be privileged, confidential, and protected by Attorney-Client privilege. Persons who are not members or employees of the Kentucky State Board of Elections should be aware of the following points: (1) You should consult your own lawyer, as I represent the SBE; (2) I am not your attorney and anything you tell me is not confidential; (3) Any information provided in an email is NOT LEGAL ADVICE, should not be relied upon as legal advice, and I do not consent to the creation of an attorney-client relationship between myself and you.  Any opinions expressed are mine alone and do not constitute policy or opinion of the SBE.  BEFORE MAKING ANY PERSONAL OR BUSINESS DECISIONS, CONSULT WITH A PRIVATE ATTORNEY."

communications.communication:

Sincerely,

Jon Smith

26-30.

*Public Records Office*

Sue Shea Martz, Analyst

communications.subject: PRR 26-30 Verra Mobility - Final and Closure
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Smith,

You requested the following records:


*"Can you please provide the number of total buses serving students with
special needs, number of total buses serving students with special needs
equipped with Verra Mobility stop arm cameras, number of total school buses
and number total buses equipped with Verra Mobility (also known as American
Traffic Solutions) stop arm cameras?*

*The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and
this request is not being made for commercial purposes.*

*In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform
me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer
the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or
CD-ROM if not."*

Our records search yielded the following information:


   - Total buses: 94
   - Total buses with stop arm cameras provided by Verra Mobility:  15
   - Total school buses (mid size & small) used for special education
   routes:  42
   - Total  school buses (mid size & small) used for special education
   routes  with stop arm cameras provided by Verra Mobility:  0


Your request is now considered complete and will be closed as of today,
March 16, 2026, with no further action required.  Please note, under RCW
42.56.550, any judicial review of the District's actions or challenges
related to public records requests must be filed within one year from
today's date.



If you have any questions about our fulfillment of your request, please
feel free to contact me.

*Public Records Office*

Sue Shea Martz, Analyst

4220 80th St NE
Marysville WA 98270

title: Bobin George Dispatch Recordings

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Bobin George Dispatch Recordings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Under the Texas Public Information Act, I request CAD records, dispatch logs, call-for-service records, call notes, incident logs, and any available 911/non-emergency audio for calls associated with phone number 832-600-0211 from September 1, 2024 through September 20, 2024, relating to Bobin George. Please include timestamps, locations, units dispatched, dispositions, and any records sufficient to identify whether the calls concerned Bobin George. Please provide records electronically and advise me first if costs will exceed $40.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bobin George

communications.subject: RE: Texas Public Information Act Request #236-2026
communications.communication:
To whom it may concern:

Under the Texas Public Information Act, I am narrowing and clarifying my prior request.

I understand your office has advised that any 911 audio recordings for this incident period no longer exist due to retention limits, and that dispatch cannot search by phone number. I am therefore requesting all remaining non-audio records relating to the incidents at 445 Westmont, Cibolo, Texas, on September 13, 2024, including:

Incident No. 2024-44106

Incident No. 2024-44128

Please provide:

Full CAD event histories and CAD printouts

Dispatcher comments, call notes, event notes, and narrative fields

Unit notes, officer notes, disposition notes, and timestamps

Log sheets for these incidents

Radio/dispatch logs or communications logs associated with these incidents

Any incident reports, supplements, or related narratives

Any records reflecting instructions or directions given by dispatch or officers to Bobin George, including any instruction to move away from the residence or to another nearby location

Any body-worn camera references, dash-cam references, report numbers, or cross-referenced records tied to these incidents

For search purposes, the relevant timeframe is approximately 12:30 PM through 4:30 PM on September 13, 2024, at 445 Westmont, Cibolo, Texas.

If any responsive records are withheld or redacted, please identify the specific statutory basis for each withholding or redaction and release all non-exempt portions.

Please provide the records electronically.

title: NCAA MFRS FY 25 Report

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Greg Chick

title: Hopsital discharge data (Department of Health)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Hopsital discharge data (Department of Health)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hospital discharge data from January 1, 2023 to January 1, 2025. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

Reference # N015325-031526. Dear Dillon Bergin, The Department of Health received a public records request from you on March 15, 2026. Your request mentioned: “To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Hospital discharge data from January 1, 2023 to January 1, 2025. 

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Dillon Bergin

title: GO-Biz: Atomic Machines, Inc. - California Competes Tax Credit (CCTC) Agreement Documentation

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: GO-Biz: Atomic Machines, Inc. - California Competes Tax Credit (CCTC) Agreement Documentation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request the following records related to the California Competes Tax Credit (CCTC) Agreement between GO-Biz and Atomic Machines, Inc. (https://www.atomicmachines.com). This agreement was widely reported and can be found online here: https://business.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/Atomic-Machines-Inc.-CCTC-Agreement.pdf

Agreement dated approximately November 2025):

Compliance Reports: All annual compliance reports, worksheets, and supporting documentation submitted by Atomic Machines, Inc. for Taxable Year 2025 (and 2026 to date) to demonstrate the achievement of milestones related to net increase of full-time employees and investment amounts.

Milestone Evaluations: Any written determinations or correspondence from GO-Biz verifying that Atomic Machines, Inc. has met its "Project/Milestone" requirements as set forth in Section 3 and Exhibit A of the Agreement.

Recapture or Breach Notices: Any records, notices, or internal memoranda discussing potential "Events of Breach" (pursuant to Section 10 of the Agreement) or the "Recapture" of tax credits due to the company’s failure to maintain hiring or investment targets.

Wage Verifications: Redacted payroll summaries or certifications provided by the company to verify it has met the "Minimum Annual Wage" and "Cumulative Average Annual Wage" commitments for California full-time employees.

Relocation Disclosures: Any communications from the company regarding its certification that, absent this credit, it would have "terminated all or a portion of its employees in California or relocate[d]... to another state.

This request is made in the public interest to ensure transparency regarding the allocation of $15,000,000 in taxpayer-funded credits - especially considering the reputation of Atomic Machine's leadership.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

communications.subject: [External Message Added] Santa Clara Police Department public records request #26-809
communications.communication:
Santa Clara Police Department ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-809: Good morning. Pursuant to your PRA request for the following information, please find our responses in bold. 1) All citation and arrest statistics for California Penal Code Section 647(b) and 653.25 for the time period of March 1, 2022, through February 28, 2026. Please provide all segregable related data, including the case number, the date and time on which the arrest or citation occurred, the officers involved, gender of those sited, DOB, race, name, and the location of the occurrence. Pursuant to government code 7922.605, we’ve provided an index of available records. Please see the attached spreadsheet. If your agency worked in collaboration with another law enforcement agency on investigations involving violations of California Penal Code Section 647(b) and 653.25, please provide the case numbers for these investigations and all relevant associated material, even if this agency was not the lead investigatory agency or the agency that executed the arrest or citation. The city has no responsive documents. 2) All police and incident reports and field interview cards associated with all citations and arrests conducted in reference to violations of 647(b) and 653.25. Please also provide all incident reports (or equivalent summaries of police interactions) related to violations of 647(b) and 653.25 for the time period of March 1, 2022, through February 28, 2026. Pursuant to government code 7922.605, we’ve provided an index of available records. Please see attached spreadsheet. The city has no responsive records for field interview cards. 3) All citation and arrest statistics for California Penal Code Section 236.1 for the time period of March 1, 2022, through February 28, 2026. Please provide all segregable related data, including the case number, the date and time on which the arrest or citation occurred, the officers involved, gender of those sited, DOB, race, name, and the location of the occurrence. The city has no responsive documents. If your agency worked in collaboration with another law enforcement agency on investigations involving violations of California Penal Code Section 653.25, please provide the case numbers for these investigations and all relevant associated material, even if this agency was not the lead investigatory agency or the agency that executed the arrest or citation. The city has no responsive documents. 4) All police and incident reports and field interview cards associated with all citations and arrests conducted in reference to violations of 236.1. Please also provide all incident reports (or equivalent summaries of police interactions) related to violations of 236.1 for the time period of March 1, 2022, through February 28, 2026. The city has no responsive documents. 5) Provide all MOUs, contracts, agreements, reports including financial documents for all prostitution diversion programs for the time period of March 1, 2022, through February 28, 2026. The city has no responsive documents. 6)Provide all MOUs, contracts, agreements, reports including financial documents for all partnerships with human trafficking task forces including all training materials for the time period of March 1, 2022, through February 28, 2026. The city has no responsive documents. All responsive documents have been uploaded and included in this message. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Santa Clara Police Department.

title: LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – (2023) Request for a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by the District including the District Responses for the year 2023

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – (2023) Request for a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by the District including the District Responses for the year 2023
communications.communication:
LAKE WASHINGTON SCHOOL DISTRICT – (2023) Request for a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by the District including the District Responses for the year 2023

Via email: sparthemer@lwsd.org

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act [RCW 42.56] I hereby request the following records:

1. I request a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests received by your agency in the year 2023 and a complete electronic copy of your agency's Responses to each of these Public Records Requests for the year 2023.

Note: Parents and students seeking GRADES and/or TRANSCRIPTS in the year 2023 can be excluded from this request.

RECORDS INSTALLMENTS - ORDER OF PRODUCTION
If production of responsive records will be completed in multiple installments, please begin by producing records in REVERSE CHRONOLOGICAL FORMAT BY DATE. The most recent public records should be produced first and the oldest records produced last by date.

DEFINITION OF RECORDS AND WRITINGS
RECORDS AND WRITINGS as defined by RCW 42.56.010(4): any handwriting, typewriting, printing, photostating, photographing, and every other means of recording any form of communication or representation including, but not limited to, letters, words, pictures, sounds, or symbols, or combination thereof, and all papers, maps, magnetic or paper tapes, photographic films and prints, motion picture, film and video recordings, magnetic or punched cards, discs, drums, diskettes, sound recordings, and other documents including existing data compilations from which information may be obtained or translated.

PRODUCTION PROCESSING AND DELIVERY OF ELECTRONIC RECORDS

Please provide all records in their native electronic format. Please do not instead create new records by printing electronic files then scanning and printing the newly scanned prints.

Washington State Law is clear that the use of 3rd party “portals” (Such as GOVQA) for a public records request is optional for the requestor. Respectfully I decline. Please provide all records electronically via direct email attachment to this email address.

Please do not register this requestor for, nor direct this requestor to the use of any 3rd party commercial portal including but not limited to FOIAOnline, GovQA, NextRequest, FOIAExpress, JustFOIA FOIADirect, WebForm, or any other online service other than a direct NO PASSWORD REQUIRED, NO REGISTRATION REQUIRED, internet cloud-based download link, or electronic email.

Do not send any portion of this public records request to any 3rd party for-profit corporation such as GOVQA, a Grannicus Corporation or NEXT-REQUEST.

Do not register this request with any for profit portal or 3rd party organization such as GOVQA, a Grannicus Corporation, or NEXT-REQUEST.

PUBLIC RECORDS REQUESTS SUBMITTED VIA EMAIL
*** FORWARD THIS PUBLIC  RECORDS REQUEST TO PUBLIC RECORDS OFFICER ***
RCW 42.56.080 - Identifiable records—Facilities for copying—Availability of public records.

"...Agencies shall honor requests received in person during an agency's normal office hours, or by mail or email, for identifiable public records unless exempted by provisions of this chapter. No official format is required for making a records request; however, agencies may recommend that requestors submit requests using an agency provided form or web page."

This public records request is being made via email. This public records request will be honored and fulfilled by your agency via email as requested or relief will be sought per statute.

NATIVE ELECTRONIC FORMAT

Please provide all identified public records in their NATIVE electronic format, either .pst file or .pdf PORTFOLIO file. Please provide all electronic records in the fewest number of individual files as per native format or use free .zip compression in each installment.

FEES
In the event that there are fees, please inform us of the total charges in advance of fulfilling the request in strict compliance with all provisions of the Washington State Public Records Act.

EXEMPTION LOGS
Please ensure all redactions or exemptions claimed by your agency in the production of responsive records are accompanied by a complete and detailed exemption log noting the valid legal reason for each exemption at each redaction location in the record produced, as well as the specific number of pages if any that your agency redacts or withholds in their entirety. Each redaction should be noted by footnote or by a clear reference to the specific justification for that redaction, and only the minimal exempt portion of any record may be withheld.

COMMERCIAL PURPOSE DECLARATION
The Washington State Public Records Act prohibits the disclosure of “lists of individuals” for a commercial purpose (RCW 42.56.070(8)). I declare this public records request is not being made for any commercial purpose whatsoever. Also, this public records request is not being made for a "list of individuals." This declaration satisfies all requirements of RCW 42.56 regarding prohibitions on lists and commercial purposes. No additional commercial purpose declaration will be completed for this public records request.

PRESERVATION OF PUBLIC RECORDS *** ANTICIPATE LITAGATION ***

WAC 44-14-03005 RETENTION OF PUBLIC RECORDS

The lawful destruction of public records is governed by retention schedules. The unlawful destruction of public records can be a crime. RCW 40.16.010 and 40.16.020. An agency is prohibited from destroying a public record, even if it is about to be lawfully destroyed under a retention schedule, if a public records request has been made for that record. RCW 42.56.100. Additional retention requirements might apply if the records may be relevant to actual or anticipated litigation. The agency is required to retain the record until the record request has been resolved.

Note: An agency can be found to violate the Public Records Act and be subject to the attorneys' fees and penalty provision if it prematurely destroys a requested record after a request is made. See Yacobellis v. City of Bellingham, 55 Wn. App. 706, 780 P.2d 272 (1989). However, it is not a violation of the Public Records Act if a record is destroyed prior to an agency's receipt of a public records request for that record. Bldg. Indus. Ass'n of Wash. v. McCarthy, 152 Wn. App. 720, 218 P.3d 196 (2009); West v. Dep't of
Nat. Res., 163 Wn. App. 238, 258 P.3d 78 (2011). The Public Records Act (chapter 42.56 RCW) and the records retention statutes (chapter 40.14 RCW) are two different laws.

COMMUNICATION VIA EMAIL

Please send all requests for clarification, status updates, and production of responsive electronic documents via direct email reply.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. We look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Good Luck.

communications.subject: Five day letter
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor,
This letter confirms that the District has received your request on today's date. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.040(1)(c) et seq., I acknowledge your request. This letter serves as the response required by the Public Records Act, RCW 42.56.520.

At this time, the District estimates that it will be able to produce these documents within the next six months.

Under the Public Disclosure Act, districts may not charge for locating documents or for inspection. The district will assess a fee of .15 cents per page for any copying. The District will email files to you and does not anticipate a fee.
If you have any questions or concerns, please contact me at 425-936-1147 or via e-mail at publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org.


Jesse Burnham, J.D.
Compliance Investigator
Risk and Safety Services, LWSD
jburnham@lwsd.org<mailto:jburnham@lwsd.org> | (425) 936-1147

communications.communication:
09-16-2024

via email to PublicRecordsRequest@lwsd.org

FORMAL REQUEST FOR REASONABLE ESTIMATE OF TIME AND COSTS  - WAC 44-06-085 RESPONSE TO PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUESTS

Hello,

 As per WAC 44-06-085 - Response to Public Records Act requests:

1. Please provide a "reasonable estimate of the time required to respond to the request" and 
2. Please "provide an estimate of copying costs" as per

WAC 44-06-085
Response to Public Records Act requests.

2(b) Reasonable estimate of time or costs. The office will provide an estimate of the time required to respond to the request, and may provide an estimate of copying costs pursuant to a specific request seeking an estimate of cost. If the requestor believes the amount of time or estimated costs stated are not reasonable, the requestor may petition the public records officer for a formal review under WAC 44-06-120.

It has now been over 5 months since the original public records request with no production of any records whatsoever.

PRESERVATION OF PUBLIC RECORDS *** ANTICIPATE LITIGATION ***

RCW 42.56.100 Protection of public records—Public access.

If a public record request is made at a time when such record exists but is scheduled for destruction in the near future, the agency, the office of the secretary of the senate, or the office of the chief clerk of the house of representatives shall retain possession of the record, and may not destroy or erase the record until the request is resolved.

NOTICE OF WRITTEN REQUEST FOR FORMAL REVIEW - PUBLIC RECORDS UNIT OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL

As per  WAC 44-06-120(4) - Review of denials of public records requests, estimates of time, estimates of costs, a request for formal review of this public records act request will be made to the Washington State Attorney General.

Thank you.

communications.subject: PRR-2024-109: installment #1
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 
  
 Thank you for submitting a public records request. Your request, PRR-2024-109 is being processed in installments: 
  
 I request a complete electronic copy of all Public Records Requests (ALL RECORDS AND WRITINGS) received by your agency in the year 2023 and a complete electronic copy of your agency's Responses (ALL RECORDS AND WRITINGS) to each of these Public Records Requests for the year 2023. 
  
 Attached is installment #1. This public records request was received 10/11/2023, request details below: 
  
 I am a freelance reporter working on a story about the seismic readiness of Washington public schools. I am writing to request a list of the Lake Washington School District buildings that have been seismically retrofitted since 1998, along with the type of retrofit, the year the retrofit was completed, and the seismic performance level that the retrofit was designed to provide (for example: risk reduction, collapse prevention, life safety, or immediate occupancy). In addition, can you please let me know which seismic performance level any newer buildings (constructed after 1998) are designed to provide? Thank you so much. 
  
 Lake Washington School District is still in the process of searching for additional records. The district is currently experiencing an unusually high volume of extensive and complex public records requests. We anticipate being able to send you the second installment by 12/20/2024. 
  
 Thank you for your patience in this regard.

On February 18, 2025, we attempted to contact you <please see attached correspondence> regarding your public records request, PRR-2024-109.

In Washington State, a public records request can be considered abandoned if the requestor fails to claim, review, access, or arrange for inspection of the requested records within 30 days of being notified that they are available.
https://app.leg.wa.gov/WAC/default.aspx?cite=44-14-04005

Regarding public records request, PRR-2024-109, the following installments have been provided:

On 12/6/2024, an installment (#1) was provided via email with ability to access documents and download.
On 12/18/2024, an installment (#2) was provided via email with ability to access documents and download.
On 1/23/2025, an installment (#3) was provided via email with attachments within the email.
On 2/3/2025, an installment (#4) was provided via email with attachments within the email.

As of 3/18/2025 these installments have not been claimed, reviewed, accessed, nor arranged for inspection. If you fail to claim the records within 30 days, or April 18, 2025, we will consider this request closed. If you request the same or almost identical records, a new public records request will need to be filed.

Thank you,
Public Records Office
15212 NE 95th ST.
Redmond, WA 98052
publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org<https://lwsdwa.justfoia.com/responsiveiframe.html?containerId=responsive_iframe_1133>




________________________________

DISCLAIMER:
Lake Washington School District Confidentiality Notice: This email message, including any attachments, may contain confidential and privileged information. Any unauthorized review, use, disclosure, or distribution of privileged information, including information protected by Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) or other provision of law, is prohibited. If you are not the intended recipient, please contact the sender by e-mail and destroy all copies of the original message.

communications.subject: Lake Washington School District, WA. - Records Request PRR-2024-109 Completed
communications.communication:
Dear Requestor, 
  
 Thank you for submitting a public records request. The request Request Number: PRR-2024-109 has been completed. Your request, PRR-2024-109, is now considered closed, as previously communicated due to the installments not being claimed, reviewed, accessed, or arranged for inspection. After 30 days from this notification, the request is considered abandoned. 
  
 In accordance with Washington State law, a public records request may be deemed abandoned if the requestor does not claim, review, access, or arrange for inspection of the requested records within 30 days of being notified of their availability. You can refer to the relevant statute here: https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sQcoFmn8lnh1asJqVwPIxu-2FfVnXzaGfHVs2dg-2BV3dh0ax1bivbr-2FDiVHDmjdPooL39zlkStNEcPxLyLLpUTBt10-3DQGuQ_NvUDiELrDjR8mh7Q7NvTdiDSnQ5v1TyneOL-2Bk5n56Bz9Yei-2FDXIe3NIVusw9j-2BSVn5aD6XDCSIpL40MZnKfH9dPV0vcwJdNlzb4zVslGq7QFWarwwAumuWtN3oXhdHlLReY8XGJCTA4joXGLw6BJZlqx4AiAFLOTLRrUvbkGq92JJPeY7lhstQ2nxL7m8gTtRCci4qrFyLo7SvbqlB2ow55HVu8tqETLDD9meCvFqhfi1hOjplNBlsvSEsxWbPaRxeh1stKZdTJv9lqstsGExINtMCcrE4vVQ50yud6dMYoNqyjeRL-2B4o8o15H-2F3MT1z

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
08-19-2025

to: PublicRecordsRequest@lwsd.org

Defective and/or missing “JUSTFOIA” download Links and continued Failure to Provide Installments of Public Records

Dear Public Records Officer,

This is a formal notice under the Washington State Public Records Act, RCW 42.56, regarding continuing serious deficiencies in your agency’s method of delivering responsive records.

The “JUSTFOIA” download links your office references are either missing or do not work/resolve. This is a complete failure to make the records “available for inspection or copying” as required by RCW 42.56.080 and RCW 42.56.520.

This issue has been repeatedly brought to your attention in prior communications across multiple public records requests. Despite these repeated notices, your office continues to provide the same missing or non-functional “JUSTFOIA” links instead of a direct, functional download method. This pattern shows ongoing noncompliance and an unwillingness to take corrective action.

Please provide, in writing:

A detailed explanation of how and why your office is unable to provide responsive records via a direct, functional download link that does not require third-party portals, registrations, or passwords.

Proof that your office has never provided such a direct download link to any member of the public in the past two years, including:

The date and recipient of each records delivery

The method of delivery used

Whether the delivery included a direct, no-registration, no-password download link

If such direct links have been provided to others in the past two years, please identify each occurrence and explain why the same method is being denied in my case.

The Public Records Act does not permit an agency to impose technical obstacles to access. If your system is incapable of providing functioning download links, you must offer an alternative delivery method that allows immediate access to the records, such as direct email attachments, a password-free cloud link, or physical media.

Please treat this as a request for public records and respond within the timelines required under RCW 42.56. Failure to remedy these delivery issues or to produce the requested explanations and proof may result in seeking judicial relief and penalties under RCW 42.56.550.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
Dear Requester,

Thank you for reaching out regarding the difficulty you're experiencing accessing public records through our JustFOIA portal.

We’ve investigated the issue and confirmed directly with JustFOIA that their system is fully operational and not experiencing any downtime or technical disruptions. Based on this, it appears that the VPN you’re using may be interfering with your ability to connect to the portal. Some VPN services route traffic through servers that are flagged or restricted by our system or by JustFOIA’s security protocols, which can result in limited or blocked access.

To help resolve this, we recommend the following steps:

Disconnect from your VPN and try accessing the portal directly through your standard internet connection.

If you require a VPN for privacy or security reasons, try switching to a different server location or protocol within your VPN settings.

Clear your browser cache or attempt access using a different browser or device.

If these steps do not resolve the issue, we’re happy to offer an alternative method of access. We can mail you a thumb drive containing the requested records. If this option works for you, please reply with your preferred mailing address and we’ll prepare the materials accordingly.

We appreciate your patience and look forward to assisting you further.

Public Records Office
15212 NE 95th Street
Redmond, Washington 98052
publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org<mailto:publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org>

communications.subject: Lake Washington School DIstrict, WA. Fee issued notification for PRR-2024-109
communications.communication:
Dear Requester, 
  
 This email concerns the public records request, Request Number: PRR-2024-109 , you submitted that was received by Lake Washington School District on Wednesday, April 10, 2024. A fee has been issued for your request for records. 
  
 Following the submission of your request, a preliminary search was completed. The estimated volume of records and associated costs are detailed in the invoice provided. District policy requires that you be provided with a summary of applicable anticipated charges before charges are imposed. This is to allow the requestor to revise the request in an effort to reduce the applicable charges. To this end, a detailed invoice is provided separately for your review. Please click here to view: Request Number: PRR-2024-109 
  
 Please be advised that District policy/procedure [https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sdDT6VWnYPCfrNw8GE28dNLFru-2BD5tK9Zn73Cfsmpb5xfQNNDYtr1dMm5vktmeCj0iSrGxGsTFS4GDzVEhwn74I-3DnWx__NvUDiELrDjR8mh7Q7NvTdiDSnQ5v1TyneOL-2Bk5n56Bz9Yei-2FDXIe3NIVusw9j-2BSVn5aD6XDCSIpL40MZnKfH9dPV0vcwJdNlzb4zVslGq7SpEYExsOp33LBYRvU4kzSzfzLCIJCzRGtvB5UzIwKaFEGY7bU3OFzSCAPgdWvyNdeeKMeWfcZuc6YNc0-2BOGwOITQYvcTS1-2BqWfBCQ4iyFZKo5BSIv0A1Gefq1CjKBAg6oR7kq0QTTb6C77uS13qrcwRSb1kRL1hSkkChd3AKvARMr3fSBKbyuOkU5wuTLFCJ6nsIRYPexqtd4nfgFYFN6g ] contains the following schedule of charges that may apply to your request: 
  
 1. Ten cents per page for public records scanned into an electronic format or for the use of District equipment to scan the records;
2. Five cents per each four electronic files or attachments uploaded to email, cloud-based data storage service, or other means of electronic delivery;
3. Ten cents per gigabyte for the transmission of public records in an electronic format or for the use of District equipment to send the records electronically; and
4. The actual cost of any digital storage media or device provided by the District, the actual cost of any container or envelope used to mail the copies to the requestor, and the actual postage or delivery charge. 
  
 Alternatively, if the District determines and documents that the fees allowed under this procedure are clearly equal to, or less, than two dollars, the District may instead charge a flat fee of two dollars to provide the records. If the District charges a flat fee for the first installment, the District will not charge an additional flat fee or a per page fee for any subsequent installments. 
  
 Due to the volume of records, the District is exercising its discretion under District procedure and RCW 42.56.120(4) to require a deposit of ten percent of the estimated charges associated with this request.  
  
 After payment is received, we will be authorized by District procedure and the Public Records Act to begin reviewing the responsive records. At that point, we will provide you with the anticipated date of an initial installment. 
  
 Please be advised that we are required to allow you a 30-day time frame to make the pre-payment as outlined above, measured from the date of this email. A failure to receive such payment from a requester within that time frame permits us to consider their request abandoned and close it. Also, the District would also be amenable to narrowing the scope of your request in an effort to reduce the associated fees the District intends to assess. 
  
 Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns about the foregoing or any aspect of the District's work to fulfill your request. 
  
 To view your request status, please click Request Number: PRR-2024-109 
  
 

Thank you, 
  
 Public Records Office 
 15212 NE 95th Street
Redmond, WA 98052 
 publicrecordsrequest@lwsd.org
