5. Policies on ALPR Use and Oversight: Any formal policies or guidelines related to: 
a. Acceptable and prohibited uses of ALPR data, including specific restrictions and permissible use cases 
b. Access policy, including what constitutes a “valid reason” for system access 
c. Human verification procedures for hotlist alerts before law enforcement action 
6. Access Logs and Audits: Any access logs, audits, compliance reports, or oversight records evaluating the use, accuracy, and policy compliance of Boulder County Sheriff's Office ALPR systems between January 1, 2021 and the present. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Matthew Spratlin 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2179756/ 
You will be contacted about the availability of records and/or provided with copies of the records. Depending on the scope of your request, a deposit and/or extension may be requested. If a deposit and/or extension is needed, Boulder County will contact you with an estimate before proceeding. All communications will be through the Boulder County Records Center. PLEASE NOTE: The Colorado Open Records Act (CORA) does not require Boulder County to create new records, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request under " My Requests" in the Boulder County Open Records Center.

communications.communication:
--- Respond above this line --- 
RE: OPEN RECORDS REQUEST March 10, 2026, Reference # C000121-031026. Dear Matthew, A modified report with the metadata you have requested can be prepared and provided at no charge. Metadata fields for [Subject Line] and [Compound Path] would be removed as these fields may contain information related to an active criminal investigation and are not subject to release pursuant to the Colorado Criminal Justice Records Act as release would be contrary to the public interest, C.R.S. 24-72-305(5).  In order to review each of the approximately 47,000 entries and corresponding metadata fields to determine which of the fields contains information related to a criminal investigation, a deposit of $ 31,440 is required. This deposit is based on the estimated staff time to manually review the large number of entries. 
Please confirm if you would like to receive the modified report or proceed with payment of the deposit.

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Banned books list (Department of Corrections)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all books and other printed publications that are banned or otherwise not permitted in prisons. Please include the title and author of the publication. If available, please include the reasons for prohibiting the publication, as well as the date of the decision.

If possible, please send this information in a searchable spreadsheet format, such as an Excel file or a CSV file.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: RE: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Banned books list (Department of Corrections)
communications.communication:
Good morning,

We are happy to announce the ODOC has upgraded to an online public records request portal, GovQA, that is now our central location to process public records requests through.  Therefore, we are no longer processing records requests that come in through resource inboxes (such as the DOC General Public Record Requests).

We kindly ask that you use the following link to create a GovQA account and submit your request.  This new portal will streamline the processing of records.

Please see the following link to GovQA to the "Submit a Records Request" area Public Records Requests | Oregon Department of Corrections<https://oregondoc.govqa.us/WEBAPP/_rs/(S(erfwg1qlil3uvveemnxer342))/supporthome.aspx>, or more general information can be found in my signature line.

Sincerely,

[cid:image001.jpg@01DC91BA.3F4567D0]
Laura Sterling
Public Records Analyst
Legal Affairs Unit
3723 Fairview Industrial Dr. SE, Suite 200
Salem, OR 97310
Cell: 971-375-5381
www.oregon.gov/DOC

The Oregon Department of Corrections has transitioned to an online public records request portal, GovQA. Please visit the ODOC website for more information: Department of Corrections : Public Record Requests : Research and Information Requests : State of Oregon<https://www.oregon.gov/doc/research-and-requests/Pages/public-records.aspx>

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Iowa Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Internal evaluation or pilot assessment reports that summarize performance, adoption, risks, or “lessons learned” from Iowa’s mDL program (Iowa MiD – IDEMIA, Samsung Wallet) between January 1, 2023 and the present.

Agreements, contracts, or memoranda of understanding with vendors or partners, including but not limited to IDEMIA, Samsung, AAMVA, or other providers.

Interagency or interstate coordination documents, including but not limited to MOUs or correspondence with AAMVA or other states regarding mDL interoperability, standards development, or airport integration at Des Moines International Airport and Eastern Iowa Airport.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rebecca Williams

communications.subject: Fees and Scope for Iowa DOT mDL request
communications.communication:
Good morning Ms. Williams,
Your request for information regarding the Iowa Mobile ID program was forwarded to me so I can assist you in obtaining the details you're seeking.  Upon initial review, it appears your request covers a broad range of information spanning multiple years. As the Iowa DOT is subject to state open records laws as well as state and federal Driver's Privacy Protection Act (DPPA) laws, all requested materials must be carefully gathered and reviewed to ensure legal compliance. Please note that fulfilling open records requests can be time-consuming. The DOT provides the first three hours of work on a data request at no charge. After that, a preparation fee of $33 per hour will apply, and this fee must be paid in advance.  To help minimize costs and expedite the process, I wanted to check in and see if there are specific pieces of information you're most interested in. Narrowing the scope of your request could significantly reduce the time and cost involved.  My team and I are here to assist you should you like to talk through what information might be most helpful to you.
Kind regards,

Angel Robinson
Director
Systems and Administration Bureau, MVD
Iowa Department of Transportation
6310 SE Convenience Blvd
Ankeny, IA 50021
515-715-5632 mobile
Angel.Robinson@iowadot.us<mailto:Angel.Robinson@iowadot.us>
Iowadot.gov
[Iowa DOT Logo]

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon, Ms. Williams,

We regret the delay in getting back to you and thank you for your understanding.

The Iowa DOT, Motor Vehicle Division, has conducted a thorough search for all relevant materials, including reports, documents, emails, agreements, and communications with both internal and external partners and vendors—such as IDEMIA, Samsung, and AAMVA.

To support your request, we are pleased to waive the fee for the first three hours of document preparation. Based on our initial assessment, we estimate that an additional 1,375 hours will be required to collect, review, and, if necessary, redact the requested materials to ensure compliance with applicable laws and privacy standards.

At the standard rate of $33 per hour for staff time, the estimated cost for this work is $45,375. In accordance with our policy, we kindly request that this amount be paid in advance before we proceed.

Please let us know how you would like to proceed or if you have any questions. We’re happy to assist in narrowing the scope of your request to reduce the time and cost involved.

Warm regards,

Trista Hills
Compliance Officer
Motor Vehicle Division, Systems & Admin Bureau
Iowa Department of Transportation
6310 SE Convenience Blvd
Ankeny, IA 50021

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Ms. Hills,

Thank you for your detailed response and for waiving the fee for the first three hours of document preparation. I appreciate the time and effort the Iowa DOT, Motor Vehicle Division, has already devoted to my request.

That said, the estimated cost of $45,375 for an additional 1,375 hours of work is prohibitive and would effectively prevent public access to these records. I want to reiterate that my request is made solely in the public interest and not for any commercial purpose. The records relate to the development and operation of digital identity and mobile driver’s license systems, which have significant implications for privacy, civil liberties, and public governance. Access to these materials will meaningfully contribute to public understanding of government operations and emerging identification technologies.

For context, other state agencies have processed similar requests and provided responsive records at no cost beyond any minimal search time. In light of that, I respectfully request that the Iowa DOT waive or substantially reduce the estimated fee so that this important public-interest request can proceed.

Please also note that my request is not withdrawn, and I ask that it remain open while this fee issue is reviewed. I am willing to work with you to identify priority categories of records or an initial subset of materials that would reduce the burden on your staff without undermining the transparency and accountability goals of the request. If you have suggestions on how best to narrow the scope while still capturing the core of the program’s agreements, policies, and vendor relationships, I would welcome your guidance.

Thank you again for your time and consideration, and for your willingness to assist in refining the request. I look forward to your response on whether the fee can be waived or significantly reduced, and on any recommended next steps for a narrower but still meaningful production.

Warm regards,
Rebecca Williams

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Hello, Ms. Williams,

Thank you for your thoughtful message and for your continued interest in the Iowa Department of Transportation’s Mobile ID program. We appreciate your recognition of the time and effort involved in responding to your request, and we remain committed to working with you to identify a path forward that balances transparency with the practical realities of staff time and resources.

In response to your willingness to narrow the scope of your request, we believe we can significantly reduce the estimated cost by focusing specifically on the release of contracts related to Iowa’s Mobile ID. This narrower scope would still provide meaningful insight into the program’s vendor relationships and operational framework, while substantially reducing the burden on our staff.

Based on this revised scope, we estimate that the time required to review and prepare the responsive materials would be approximately 35 hours, resulting in a total fee of $1,155. This includes the waiver of the first three hours of preparation time, as previously noted.

We hope this adjusted approach aligns with your goals of promoting public understanding and accountability, while also making the request more feasible to fulfill. Please let us know if you would like to proceed with this narrowed scope, or if you have additional refinements in mind.

We value your partnership in this process and welcome your response.

Sincerely,

Trista Hills
Compliance Officer
Motor Vehicle Division, Systems & Admin Bureau
Iowa Department of Transportation
6310 SE Convenience Blvd
Ankeny, IA 50021
[cid:image001.png@01DC5ADF.E6E13D00]

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Hills,

Thank you for the thoughtful response and for continuing to work with me on this request.

I would like to better understand the basis for the revised estimate of 35 hours for the review and preparation of contracts alone. Because these are finalized procurement records, I want to confirm a few points before proceeding.

Specifically, could you please let me know:

Whether the Mobile ID contracts are already maintained as final, executed documents within a centralized procurement or contract management system;

Whether these contracts are already publicly available, in whole or in part, through the Iowa DOT website, the State of Iowa procurement portal, or another public-facing repository; and

Whether the estimated time reflects substantive legal review beyond standard confirmation of exemptions.

To clarify my request further in an effort to reduce or eliminate review time, I am seeking final, executed Mobile ID–related contracts only, produced as they exist, and I am not requesting redaction of standard contractual terms. If there are limited portions that the Department believes are exempt, I am amenable to those portions being withheld rather than undertaking extensive line-by-line redaction.

If the contracts are already publicly posted, links to those records would fully satisfy my request.

Given the nature of these records as core public procurement documents, I am concerned that a 35-hour estimate may substantially overstate the work required. I would appreciate clarification on whether the scope can be adjusted to allow for production with minimal or no additional staff time.

Thank you again for your engagement, and I look forward to your guidance on how best to proceed.

Sincerely,
Rebecca Williams

communications.subject: RE: Iowa Open Records Law Request: IA mDL Program Records (Jan 2023–Present)

We appreciate your continued patience as we work to refine the documentation to include only the materials most relevant to your request. To ensure we have a clear understanding of your needs, we would like to schedule a Teams meeting with you. Please share a few dates and times that work best for your schedule.

Thank you,
Trista

Dear Ms. Williams,

Thank you for your patience. Attached you will find documentation responsive to your inquiry. Please note that, pursuant to Iowa Code 22.7, certain information has been redacted. If you need any additional information, please feel free to reach out. This request has been marked as completed.

Respectfully,

communications.subject: FOIA Recieved 2205
communications.communication:
Greetings,

The Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office has received your request for public records and is processing your request. All requests are processed in the order in which they are received, and no requests are prioritized by deadline date. The amount of time to completely process your request depends upon the number of pending requests, volume of material encompassed in the request, and the amount of redaction, if any, that is necessary.

Please be aware, if you are requesting records with large amount of body camera videos and or high-profile cases, the time to complete your request may be significantly longer as these cases are often voluminous. Identifying the specific record(s) being requested may help expedite your request. You will be notified once records are made available for you to download or pick up, or if no records are found. Your tracking # is 2205. When checking the status of your request, please send emails to mediarequest@mcso.maricopa.gov<mailto:mediarequest@mcso.maricopa.gov> and include your tracking #.

***Pursuant to ARS 39-129, the Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office (MCSO) Legal Liaison Section has implemented a fee schedule for requests for video recordings.  The fee for processing video recordings is $25 per each video-hour ($6.25 per quarter hour).  In the event there are contemporaneous multiple requests for the same video, the cost associated with processing the video will be shared equally between all requesting parties at that time. Requests for previously processed videos will be at no cost unless the requestor wishes to receive a hard copy instead of an electronic copy. Fees for hardcopies are listed below. ***

The fee breakdown for hard copies are as follows:

Documents:                       $.50 for each page

CD’s:                                      $15.00 each

Thank you

—
Calbert Gillett
[Sent from Front]

communications.subject: RE: Arizona Public Records Law Request #2205
communications.communication:
Hi Calbert,

Thank you for your response to my Arizona Public Records Law request regarding records referencing “Flipper Zero.” I appreciate the effort involved in conducting the search.

However, I note that the records provided are entirely redacted. While I understand that certain portions of responsive records may be sensitive, Arizona Public Records Law requires that exemptions be narrowly applied, specifically identified, and justified, and that any reasonably segregable, non-exempt portions be released.

To that end, I respectfully request the following:
1) Identification of the specific statutory exemptions relied upon for the redactions (not generalized descriptions such as “law enforcement sensitive” or “confidential”).
2) A brief explanation of how those exemptions apply to the withheld material.
3) Confirmation that a segregability review was conducted, and an explanation if the agency believes no portion of the records can be reasonably segregated and released.

To facilitate release and reduce any legitimate risk concerns, I am expressly not seeking:
- Personally identifying information of officers, staff, or suspects
- Case-specific investigative details
- Duplicate information (it appears that documents/emails may have been found in more than one individual's inbox. You may de-duplicate)

My request is limited to policy-level, administrative, or general discussions referencing the Flipper Zero device. The device is widely known and routinely discussed in publicly available sources, including mainstream media, technology and security communities, and publicly accessible court filings. Given this broad public availability, references to the device do not, by themselves, reveal investigative techniques or sensitive law enforcement information.

Arizona courts have consistently disfavored blanket redactions and conclusory assertions of sensitivity without specific legal justification. If the agency continues to withhold the records in full, please treat this correspondence as a request for a formal denial that identifies the precise legal grounds for each category of withholding, so that I may evaluate further review options.

I am hopeful this can be resolved cooperatively and look forward to your response.

Sincerely,
AJS

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2208082/" I am the person assigned to fulfill your request.  The Public Records Act obliges the County to attend to your request in a manner commensurate with our other duties and obligations. I will be coordinating with other County offices and departments in the collection, review, and production of responsive records. The County will charge requestors per its policy in a manner that is reasonable, cost-efficient, and fair to the requestor.  Such charges will include fees for copying and/or transmitting records.  When a records request is for a large volume of records, the County may elect to provide records on an installment basis. You will not be charged any fees related to this request until you are provided with a cost estimate, either an installment estimate or estimate of the anticipated cost for the full request, whichever is more applicable. Producing records in installments ensures the County provides you with the fullest assistance and most timely production of records. We anticipate we will be able to provide you with the records or a first installment of records on or before 3/5/26  If there are fees associated with providing you with the records, you will be provided a cost estimate. You will then have an opportunity to amend or withdraw your request if you wish to avoid paying the accompanying fees. Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2208082/" 
An installment of records responsive to your request is available to you now in the Lewis County Public Records Center. Please login to the Public Records Center and retrieve/download the records.  Please note that documents may be redacted pursuant to and in compliance with RCW 42.56 and other applicable statues when produced. 
The County will continue to attempt to locate additional responsive records (if any) and we anticipate providing a status of our search results or an additional installment to you on or before 4/2/26  Producing records in installments ensures the County provides you with the fullest assistance and most timely production of records.  If you have not received a response in that time, please do not hesitate to contact me directly inquiring as to the status. Of course, if we locate responsive records prior to this date we will advise you promptly.  Conversely, if the County encounters a need to extend our estimate, you will be contacted with a revised estimated date. Please be aware, to prevent excessive interference with our other essential functions and per our policy, the County will not process future installments until the released records are retrieved by you.  If the records are not accessed within 30 days of their availability, the request will be automatically closed as an abandoned request. 
Thank you for your continued patience while we process your request. 
Sincerely, 
Cherish Willis 

 
If you have never used the Lewis County Public Records Center before, your email address will be your login.  You may choose the "forgot password" option to obtain a password for your use.

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P016723-020326
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
  
   
 Public Disclosure Office  
 351 NW North Street, Chehalis, WA 98532 RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of February 03, 2026, Reference # P016723-020326 
Dear Rose Terse, 
Lewis County received a public records request from you on February 03, 2026. You requested the following: 
"To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2208082/" After a diligent search, records responsive to your request have been released to the Lewis County Public Records Center. To access the records login to the Public Records Center to retrieve/download the records.  Please note that documents may be redacted pursuant to and in compliance with RCW 42.56 and other applicable statutes when produced. Per our policy, the County is waiving the fees for providing responsive records regarding this particular request since the cost would be less than one dollar.  In the future please be aware, if applicable, the fees for records will be imposed for future requests. Lewis County's Public Records Policy and fee schedule can be found on the County’s website at: http://lewiscountywa.gov/departments/public-records/ 
The County considers this particular request fulfilled and is now closed. The PRA’s one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run and the County does not intend to further address this request because of the closure. If you believe we have misunderstood your request or the scope of your request or believe additional responsive records have not been provided, please let us know as soon as possible or within 30 days as it is our intent to work with you to provide the records you are requesting. 
Thank you for your patience while we processed your request. 
Sincerely, Cherish Willis Public Records Specialist (360) 740-1299 http://lewiscountywa.gov/departments/public-records/ 
To monitor the progress or update this request please log into the Public Records Center. 
If you have never used the Lewis County Public Records Center before, your email address will be your login.  You may choose the "forgot password" option to obtain a password for your use.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Humboldt County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Humboldt County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: USCIS Litigation Guidance

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: USCIS Litigation Guidance
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any policy, procedure, guidance, manual, or similar agency record reflecting the order of operations and loci of responsibility within USCIS upon receiving notice that a visa applicant or petitioner has filed suit against the agency in federal court challenging its failure to timely process or make a final decision on a petition or application. 

This request does NOT seek: 

- any case-specific information regarding specific litigation 
- any records pertaining to district court actions challenging naturalization denials pursuant to the provisions of the INA providing federal judicial review as a matter of right. 
- any specific legal advice regarding a particular issue from DOJ to USCIS, or within USCIS. 

Rather, this request seeks records shedding light on what happens logistically between the Department of Justice and USCIS to process litigation information and determine who, where, and how decisions will be made regarding the defense of these suits. 

Please task a time period including January 20, 2009 to the present. 

USCIS Office of the Chief Counsel and the USCIS Director’s office are believed to be the most likely primary custodians of record, but USCIS’s domestic field offices should also be tasked in the event there is not formalized, national litigation response policy. 

I make this request on behalf of the Al Otro Lado /#DetentionKills Transparency Initiative.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Hoppock

title: USCIS Litigation Guidance

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: USCIS Litigation Guidance
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any policy, procedure, guidance, manual, or similar agency record reflecting the order of operations and loci of responsibility within USCIS upon receiving notice that a visa applicant or petitioner has filed suit against the agency in federal court challenging its failure to timely process or make a final decision on a petition or application. 

This request does NOT seek: 

- any case-specific information regarding specific litigation 
- any records pertaining to district court actions challenging naturalization denials pursuant to the provisions of the INA providing federal judicial review as a matter of right. 
- any specific legal advice regarding a particular issue from DOJ to USCIS, or within USCIS. 

Rather, this request seeks records shedding light on what happens logistically between the Department of Justice and USCIS to process litigation information and determine who, where, and how decisions will be made regarding the defense of these suits. 

Please task a time period including January 20, 2009 to the present. 

USCIS Office of the Chief Counsel and the USCIS Director’s office are believed to be the most likely primary custodians of record, but USCIS’s domestic field offices should also be tasked in the event there is not formalized, national litigation response policy. 

I make this request on behalf of the Al Otro Lado /#DetentionKills Transparency Initiative.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

title: FBI Memos Trump Epstein (Department of Justice, Office of the Attorney General)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI Memos Trump Epstein (Department of Justice, Office of the Attorney General)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the FBI memos referenced in this New York Times story: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/25/us/politics/trump-epstein-files.html Specifically, I seek a copy of any and all "F.B.I. memos summarizing interviews the bureau did in connection to claims made in 2019 by a woman who came forward after Mr. Epstein’s arrest to say she had been sexually assaulted by both Mr. Trump and Mr. Epstein decades earlier, when she was a minor." The FBI wrote four such memos, according to the New York Times report, but three of them have not been released publicly. We seek to obtain those three memos. For more information about this case, see the attached document. 

I am a reporter for The Nation magazine and other news outlets, and seek a fee waiver. If that waiver is denied, I am willing to pay $25 for these records.

I seek expedited processing for this request. The information I seek could shed light on a matter of urgent and compelling public interest -- namely, the potential involvement of federal government personnel in the alleged sex trafficking operation organized by the late Jeffrey Epstein. This information is at the center of public discourse at this moment, and the Epstein case is a matter that involves the White House, Congress and numerous judicial bodies. It is imperative that the public obtain information on this matter in a timely matter, particularly given the corrosive effects the Epstein case has had on trust in government. For the reasons above, I ask that my request be given expedited processing because there is an urgency to inform the public about actual or alleged federal government activity, and I am a person primarily engaged in disseminating information. I intend to use the requested records to inform the public about actual government activity. I certify the foregoing to be true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wilkes County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wilkes County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Whitfield County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
Good morning,

I am in receipt of your request.  Please note that our agency only became equipped with a tracking system for Use of Force in 2021.  Spreadsheets for the UOF are available, however, not in the format as you have listed in your request.  UOF reports, which provide further detail, may also be provided.  There will be a charge to fulfill this request.  The first fifteen (15) minutes of research are free.  A fee of twenty dollars ($20.00) an hour will accrue for further.

O.C.G.A. §50-18-71 Right of access; timing; fees; denial of requests; impact of electronic records
(c)

  *   (1) An agency may impose a reasonable charge for the search, retrieval, redaction, and production or copying costs for the production of records pursuant to this article. An agency shall utilize the most economical means reasonably calculated to identify and produce responsive, nonexcluded documents. Where fees for certified copies or other copies or records are specifically authorized or otherwise prescribed by law, such specific fee shall apply when certified copies or other records to which a specific fee may apply are sought. In all other instances, the charge for the search, retrieval, or redaction of records shall not exceed the prorated hourly salary of the lowest paid full-time employee who, in the reasonable discretion of the custodian of the records, has the necessary skill and training to perform the request; provided, however, that no charge shall be made for the first quarter hour.

Please let me know if you would like to proceed further with the request.


Thank you,

Marcy Cochran
Internal Affairs Coordinator
Whitfield County Sheriff’s Office
805 Professional Boulevard
Dalton, GA 30720
mcochran@whitfieldcountyga.com<mailto:mcochran@whitfieldcountyga.com>
Direct:  706-876-1429
Main:  706-278-3029

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Lafayette Parish Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Lafayette Parish Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Banned books list (Department of Corrections)

communications.subject: South Carolina Public Records Law Request: Banned books list (Department of Corrections)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the South Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all books and other printed publications that are banned or otherwise not permitted in prisons. Please include the title and author of the publication. If available, please include the reasons for prohibiting the publication, as well as the date of the decision.

If possible, please send this information in a searchable spreadsheet format, such as an Excel file or a CSV file.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: Summers, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Summers, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Kylie Frederick
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
Mar. 12 2026

Lynn Reed, County Clerk
120 Ballengee Street, Suite 106
Hinton, WV 25951

Dear, Lynn Reed
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Summers County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting:
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you for considering my request.
Sincerely, Kylie Frederick
kyliejfrederick04@gmail.com

communications.subject: Re: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Summers, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:

If you are not the intended recipient, please contact the sender by reply e-mail and destroy all copies of the original message.

communications.subject: Welcome to the Public Records Center!
communications.communication:
Dear Rose  Terse, 
 
Thank you for registering with the Public Records Center.

communications.subject: Police Department Records Request :: S019856-021826
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse: 
Thank you for your interest in public records of City of Marysville Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. Your request was received in this office on 2/18/2026 and given the reference number S019856-021826 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Mission documentation of each UAS (Unmanned Aircraft System, also known as "drone") deployment in 2025, sufficient to determine the date, time, location, and purpose of each deployment. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2216687/ 
Not all public documents are available in electronic format. If the document(s) requested are not available electronically, we will make them available for inspection or by paper copy in accordance with the Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW. 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the relevant department(s) to locate the information you seek and to determine the volume and any costs associated with satisfying your request. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question. PLEASE NOTE: The State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Public Records Center. 
City of Marysville Police Department

title: BWC for Jessica Copeland

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: BWC for Jessica Copeland
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I hope you are doing well.
I am requesting the following records related to this case:
	•	Body-worn camera (BWC) footage
	•	CCTV / surveillance footage
	•	Incident reports
	•	Investigation footage
Case details:
	•	Name: Jessica Copeland
	•	Incident / Arrest Date: August 6, 2025

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Martin Brooks

Thank you,


Nicole Bennett

title: Rhode Island HHS response

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Rhode Island HHS response
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Rhode Island Department of Health remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request internal communications between any Rhode Island Department of Health employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

I additionally request that, if possible, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.subject: RE: 26-137: RI Access to Public Records Act Request: Rhode Island HHS response
communications.communication: The Rhode Island Department of Health (“RIDOH”) is in receipt of your request, pursuant to R.I. Gen. Laws §§ 38-2-1 et seq. (the “Access to Public Records Act”). Please be advised that, because of the number of requests pending, RIDOH requires additional time to respond to your request and, pursuant to R.I. Gen. Laws §38-2-3, RIDOH may take up to twenty (20) additional business days  to comply with your request.

communications.subject: RE: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request #26-137
communications.communication:
Ileana,
Please see attached, in response to your records request.

The records deemed public in the possession of the Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH) are attached hereto. We note that some of the records you requested are deemed not to be public pursuant to (i) R.I. Gen. Laws § 38-2.2(4)(A)(1)(a), which protects information subject to attorney-client privilege from disclosure, and § 38-2-2(4)(K) which protects preliminary drafts, notes, impressions, memoranda, working papers, and work products.

R.I. Gen. Laws § 38-2-8 requires we provide you notice of your right to appeal this response to the Director of Health, Attention Legal Services, Three Capitol Hill, Providence, RI  02908, file a complaint with the Department of the Attorney General, 150 South Main Street, Providence, Rhode Island, 02903, or file a complaint with the Rhode Island Superior Court of the county where the relevant records are maintained.

Further information concerning the Access to Public Records Act is available through the Attorney General’s website at www.riag.ri.gov<http://www.riag.ri.gov>

Joseph Wendelken
Public Information Officer
Rhode Island Department of Health
